purpose: Machine-readable assets, equipment, inventory-adjacent catalog, site/geodata, dispatch, owner/operator, transfer/signoff, maintenance lifecycle, search parity, and asset-cost contract for the platform-maturity ultragoal. This gate proves repo-local implementation, test, route ownership, RLS/audit, and browser contract coverage before live rollout or analytics claims.
nonClaimPolicy: G006 may prove local contract and regression coverage for asset/dispatch lifecycles. Production Argo rollout, live DB/API/browser equipment counts, map screenshots, and live data import parity remain G009; any route audit screenshotTraceEvidence that is still Pending must be treated as a rollout blocker, not as live closure.

routePaths.requiredStory: group admin manages customer/site/address/geodata records with bounded org scope, coordinate pairing, and map readiness without fabricating missing locations
authorized users browse all visible equipment, search by chassis/equipment number, management number, model, VIN, customer, and site, then open detail or manage actions
equipment detail shows identity, owner/operator assignment, lifecycle events, relationship graph, work-order links, and catalog actions for a single source object
group admin can see the equipment list and create/edit/decommission from one management surface while selecting the legal owner organization with signoff
legacy master-list equipment import preserves raw fields and routes data into the equipment registry without bypassing typed lifecycle validation
location/geofence settings capture address, province, city, coordinate pair, geofence radius, arrival/departure readiness, and privacy/consent boundaries
dispatch board supports selected work-order detail, assignment, P1 broadcast, accept/decline, force assign, SLA risk, and object-linked dispatch history
map view loads equipment-by-location, site assets, ungeocoded-site handling, arrival/departure events, and scoped asset panels without crashing on partial GPS data
inspection/preventive maintenance links equipment picker, cycle, schedule, checklist, and lifecycle evidence to the equipment source object
KPI-tier user browses ontology object types, expands link relationships, and traces an equipment object instance to its source object within enforced group/org scope

dependencyRoutes.requiredStory: asset and dispatch operations must deep-link to the work-order detail lifecycle already enforced by G005
asset sales/acquisition/rental economics and lifecycle-cost analysis remain tied to equipment source objects before G008 finance maturity claims
general inventory and item-type extensibility are represented through catalog/object-type lineage until the governed import/export lane closes

backendContracts.contract: Registry REST exposes list/detail/create/update/import/by-location/substitution/timeline/ownership-transfer routes, and mutating equipment/site paths are gated by EquipmentManage.
Registry application models legal-owner transfer as an ordered signoff workflow while equipment list queries search by management number, model, maker, equipment number, customer, site, and VIN.
Registry adapter performs audited writes, normalized search, equipment CRUD/import/substitution, and durable ownership-transfer request/decision persistence before mutating asset owner state.
Registry equipment master stores equipment number, management number, VIN, asset owner, status indexes, and source fields as tenant-scoped source objects.
Equipment autocomplete/search is indexed for management number, equipment number, and model so chassis/unit-number search parity is not a full-table scan after import.
Site geodata requires address, province/city, paired WGS84 coordinates, bounded text, and a geocoded-site partial index instead of fabricated map positions.
Site geofence radius is bounded and nullable, inheriting registry site RLS for arrival/departure detection without new cross-org exposure.
Asset acquisition cost/date are first-class accounting facts on equipment master and explicitly distinct from depreciation vehicle value.
Equipment ownership transfer is durable, RLS-isolated, append-only/no-delete, and ordered through sending org, receiving org, legal, and accounting signoff.
requiredSnippets: equipment ownership transfer requests are a durable approval ledger
equipment ownership transfer events are append-only

backendContracts.contract: P1 dispatch domain is a finite-state machine with broadcast, accept-window, response, and auto-assignment guards.
requiredSnippets: dispatch responses are only allowed while broadcasting

backendContracts.contract: Dispatch REST exposes start/respond/get/force-assign routes and schedules follow-up jobs with tenant context for alerts/escalation.
Dispatch adapter enforces priority P1, GPS freshness, target availability, audited responses, force assignment, alert leases, and RLS-scoped persistence.
requiredSnippets: P1 dispatch requires work-order priority P1

backendContracts.contract: Asset lifecycle economics are deterministic: TCO resolves acquisition basis, adds maintenance exactly once, computes margin only when sold, and avoids divide-by-zero rates.
requiredSnippets: Gross margin on a realized sale

backendContracts.contract: Financial lifecycle summary reads equipment master, maintenance cost ledger, outsource works, sale listing, and timeline under RLS to support later sell/keep/rental analysis.

frontendContracts.contract: Equipment page allows group-admin owner-org selection and delegates create/edit flows through group tenant context instead of silently writing to the current HQ org.
Equipment management combines browse/list context with create/edit/import/site/substitution management so users select equipment from the list before managing it.
Management panel supports legal owner selection, delegated group context, create/edit/decommission, and ownership-transfer request with signoff acknowledgement.
Group-admin subsidiary options flatten group membership so asset owner selection can target any authorized subsidiary.
Equipment browse is the primary searchable table: equipment number, management number, status, model, customer/site, sorting, row-open detail, and manage actions stay visible.
Equipment detail is the source-object cockpit for identity, assignment, lifecycle events, relationship graph, links, and generated action catalog.
Dispatch page binds dispatch board selection to work-order dispatch controls, P1 broadcast, force assign, and mechanic accept/decline offers.
Dispatch controls expose priority, schedule, assignment, force-assign confirmation, and P1 dispatch actions from the source object.
Dispatch map loads equipment-by-location and arrival/departure events, handles ungeocoded/partial data, and exposes a site asset panel for authorized managers.
Site geography panel edits address/province/city/coordinates/geofence and refreshes equipment-by-location for map readiness.
Substitution panel supports reserve/replacement policy by choosing down equipment and candidate substitutes from site or search result pools.
Asset lifecycle cost panel surfaces TCO, maintenance spend, sale margin, and decision signal for later operations optimization without hiding missing acquisition data.

safetyAssertions: Legal owner and operating customer/site are separate concepts; changing legal owner requires ordered sender, receiver, legal, and accounting signoff.
Group admin equipment creation must select the target subsidiary owner org and write under delegated group context instead of the signed-in HQ org by default.
KNL operation of affiliate-owned equipment must preserve legal owner/operator distinction and be represented as policy-bound operating scope, not raw org mutation.
Equipment search must cover equipment number, management number/호기 with leading-zero normalization, model, maker, VIN, customer, and site.
Equipment management and browse flows must keep the whole list visible and let users select an equipment row to manage rather than requiring a disconnected separate screen.
Cross-org ownership transfer and dispatch/maintenance lifecycle decisions must be auditable and terminal states must not be silently overwritten.
Sites with missing coordinates are listed as ungeocoded; maps must not invent GPS coordinates, and coordinate pairs/geofence bounds are validated.
P1 dispatch requires priority P1, fresh GPS/target readiness, response within the broadcast window, and auditable force assignment/escalation.
Asset lifecycle economics must remain deterministic and source-object based: acquisition, maintenance cost, outsource work, sale price, margin, and TCO are visible for future optimization.

Inventory and item-type extensibility use ontology/catalog/import lineage until governed import/export closes in G008; G006 cannot claim arbitrary inventory maturity beyond equipment/spares/substitutions.
Live equipment counts, browser screenshots, map traces, and production data parity remain G009 unless captured by production evidence.
